text.title: lang-jvm-jreleaser-tag-release Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for lang-jvm-jreleaser-tag-release. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every JVM library or CLI release MUST be triggered by a git tag matching v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+(-rc[0-9]+)? and execute exactly one CI job invoking ./mvnw -DskipTests -Prelease deploy && jreleaser full-release (or the Gradle equivalent). Manual mvn deploy, manual GitHub Release creation, and manual CHANGELOG.md edits in the release commit are forbidden. AI agents may bump the version in source files, but the publish step must be the workflow.
rule.rationale: Tag-as-trigger gives a single, auditable provenance line ("commit X tagged v1.4.0 produced artifacts Y") and removes every step a human (or agent) might forget. Per JReleaser docs and JEP-style "one source of truth" guidance.

rule.statement: jreleaser.yml MUST configure deploy.maven.mavenCentral.sonatype with url: https://central.sonatype.com/api/v1/publisher and applyMavenCentralRules: true. Signing MUST be enabled (signing.active: ALWAYS, signing.armored: true, with GPG key + passphrase in env vars / OIDC, never in the file). Agents are forbidden from setting signing.active: NEVER to "fix" a CI failure.
rule.rationale: OSSRH-targeted configs deploy nowhere in 2026; Central Portal rules (POM completeness, sources/javadoc jars, signed checksums) are stricter than legacy OSSRH and JReleaser bakes them in.
